I want to build a digital tasbeeh i.e. a counter app for iOS and Android. Begin with the homepage of the app. Build this with a modular architecture, as I will add Supabase authentication, a store for purchasing themes, and other pages in future prompts.

Background:
The app will have various themes users can purchase, so create a global theming system that controls the background image and global text color. I will setup the themes later, for now consider we are using the default theme. The app background will be labelled AppBackground in the code, and global text colour will be labelled GlobalTextColour in the code. For the default theme, AppBackground will be solid Hex #000080,  and GlobalTextColour will be Hex #FFFFFF.
Set the global typography of the app to use the system default font of the platform.
The attached “1-Default Theme Main Page” is the ‘Home Page’ design of the app. Design the app accordingly.
1)	The Counter Engine: Create a counter with a circle, label it as ‘CounterCircle’. At the center of this circle, display the ‘CounterValue’. The CounterValue will range from “0” to “999,999”.
2)	 Interaction: Inside the CounterCircle is the ‘CounterCircleArea’. This will be the only area which, when pressed/tapped, will increase the CounterValue by “+1”. If the CounterCircle is pressed and held, it will still change CounterValue by “+1”. If the CounterCircle is pressed more than 7 times per second, the CounterValue will not change and a prompt in glassmorphism type will appear with the warning “Slow down and focus on your Dhikr for maximum benefit”. The user can close the prompt or tap anywhere else outside it to close. 
3)	Overflow Logic: If the CounterValue is at the maximum value of 999,999 and the CounterCircleArea is pressed/tapped again, the CounterValue must reset to 0, and subsequent taps add +1. We will refer to this entire feature together as 'TheCounter'.
Below the Counter Circle, the “Reduce Button” will be on the right and will be a “-“ symbol, and the Reset Button” on the left will be a reload symbol. “Reset Button and “Reduce Button” will be transparent, with the texts and symbols inside them the same colour as GlobalTextColour for the theme. Add a thick, solid border to ‘CounterCircle’ to act as an outer ring, with the same colour as GlobalTextColour.
When the “Reduce Button” is tapped, decrease the counter value by “-1”. The CounterValue can never go below “0”. If the Reset button is pressed, set the CounterValue to “0”. Touching all buttons would count as tapping the buttons just once only.

Below the Reset and Reduce buttons, add a menu button called ‘DhikrSelector’. The text inside this button will be labelled ‘DhikrSelectorText’. 

There will be two arrows on either side of the DhikrCircle as in the reference photo. Label the left one as CounterArrowLeft and right one as CounterArrowRight. Label their colour code as "CounterArrowColour", and it will be the same as GlobalTextColour.

Above the Counter Circle, create an area with 3 lines. The first line is “DhikrArabic”, the line below it is “DhikrTransliteration”, and the bottom line is “DhikrMeaning”. Center all 3 lines.
At the initial stage of UI designing, use the following set to include the corresponding texts in the DhikrSelectorText area:

DhikrSelectorText: SubhanAllah | DhikrArabic: سُبحانَ الله| DhikrTransliteration: SubhanAllah | DhikrMeaning: Glory be to Allah

Add a hamburger menu on the top left which would be the menu button.

- Default theme: Navy Blue background (#000080) with White text (#FFFFFF)
- Modular architecture ready for future theme purchases from the store

**2. Counter Engine (TheCounter):**
- Counter circle with values from 0 to 999,999 (formatted with commas)
- Tap/press to increment by +1
- Press and hold also increments by +1 (single tap)
- Tap rate limiting: Max 7 taps per second
- Glassmorphism warning modal when tapping too fast
- Overflow logic: Resets to 0 after reaching 999,999

**3. UI Elements:**
- **DhikrArabic**: سُبحانَ الله (Arabic text)
- **DhikrTransliteration**: SubhanAllah
- **DhikrMeaning**: Glory be to Allah
- **CounterCircle**: Large circle with thick white border
- **CounterArrowLeft & CounterArrowRight**: Navigation arrows on both sides
- **Reset Button**: Reload icon (resets counter to 0)
- **Reduce Button**: Minus icon (decrements by -1, never goes below 0)
- **DhikrSelector**: Button at the bottom showing "SubhanAllah"
- **Hamburger Menu**: Top left menu button

**4. Modular Architecture:**
- `contexts/ThemeContext.tsx`: Global theme management
- `hooks/useCounter.ts`: Counter logic separated from UI
- `components/WarningModal.tsx`: Reusable warning modal
- Platform-specific files for iOS and Android
- Ready for future additions (Supabase auth, theme store, etc.)

**5. Technical Features:**
- System default fonts (no custom fonts needed)
- Atomic JSX pattern (one variable per Text component)
- Console logging for debugging user actions
- Cross-platform compatibility (iOS & Android)

*   **Dhikr Display:** Above the counter, you'll see three lines displaying the Dhikr in Arabic, its transliteration, and its meaning, starting with "SubhanAllah". You'll also find left and right arrows next to the counter, matching your global text color.
*   **Navigation & Selection:** A `DhikrSelector` button is present below the counter controls, ready for future selection of different Dhikrs. Additionally, a hamburger menu icon is available in the top-left corner for future navigation options.

- This triggered re-renders and state updates during the render phase

## ✅ Changes Completed:

### 1. **Purged Ghost Code (Custom Header Hacks)**
- Removed all custom header implementations from HomeScreen and ThemesScreen
- Deleted manual hamburger menu positioning and custom header containers

### 2. **Configured Native Stack Header & Back Button**
- Updated `app/_layout.tsx` with:
  - `headerShown: true`
  - `headerTransparent: true`
  - `headerStyle: { backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.65)', elevation: 0, shadowOpacity: 0, borderBottomWidth: 0 }`
  - `headerBackVisible: true` - Native OS back arrow now automatically renders on ThemesScreen

### 3. **Safely Applied Theme Colors**
- Used `useTheme()` hook inside HomeScreen and ThemesScreen components
- Applied `navigation.setOptions({ headerTintColor: GlobalTextColour })` inside `useEffect` to dynamically color the header title and back arrow
- Added text shadows to header titles for readability

### 4. **Dynamic Padding Fix**
- Imported `useHeaderHeight` from `@react-navigation/elements` in both HomeScreen and ThemesScreen
- Applied `paddingTop: headerHeight` to the outermost container of both screens
- This ensures the CounterCircle and Theme Cards sit perfectly below the native header's touch zone on all devices (S22 Ultra, iPhone, etc.)

### 5. **Themes Screen Integrity**
- Maintained the activeTheme background rendering (color or image)
- Preserved the absolute fill `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.75)` overlay for proper contrast
- Applied dynamic padding to prevent content from being hidden under the header

## 🔍 Verified:
- ✅ API endpoints: Not applicable (no backend calls in this change)
- ✅ File links: All imports verified (`@react-navigation/elements`, `useNavigation`, `useHeaderHeight`)
- ✅ Platform-specific files: Updated both `index.tsx` and `index.ios.tsx` with identical logic
- ✅ Native back button: Enabled via `headerBackVisible: true` in Stack configuration
- ✅ Header transparency: Semi-transparent dark background `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.65)` applied
- ✅ Dynamic padding: `useHeaderHeight` ensures content sits below header touch zone on all devices

The native Stack navigation header is now fully restored with a polished semi-transparent dark background, the native back button is enabled for ThemesScreen, and all content has proper dynamic padding to prevent touch issues on Android and iOS devices!
